sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 0.9g of fat. saturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and omega3 are doing most of the work. for a legume, stable carbs plus protein is the dream.
sections.subtitleText: 0.9g of fat. saturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and omega3 are doing most of the work. for a legume, stable carbs plus protein is the dream.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 63.3g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.
sections.subtitleText: 63.3g of carbs. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 21.6g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.
sections.subtitleText: 21.6g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: magnesium at 30% daily value, iron at 29% daily value, potassium at 32% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.
sections.subtitleText: magnesium at 30% daily value, iron at 29% daily value, potassium at 32% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first: cheap staple with real payoff. this matters because a food is stronger when people can actually use it often. filling for the calories. legumes usually earn a lot of practical value from satiety. one of the cheaper ways to buy useful nutrition. that matters for real diets, not just theory.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides: can take planning or prep. that is a small but real friction cost. prep time can still be a barrier. dry legumes especially ask more from the cook. texture is not universally loved. that can reduce how often people actually use them.

closing.summary: Black Beans has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
